sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports managing through depression and experiencing asthma exacerbations during pollen season. He reports knee pain from running and lightheadedness when walking up stairs. He also reports eating on the go with junk food and traveling to sports tournaments. The patient reports feeling dizzy and lightheaded.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient's blood pressure is within normal limits. His heart rate is regular and within the normal range. Lung auscultation reveals bilateral expiratory wheezing. The examination reveals no cervical lymphadenopathy or thyroid enlargement. The abdomen is soft and non-tender. Lower extremities are without edema.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's primary problems include depression, asthma, and diabetes. He is currently taking prozac and metformin.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Order an increase in metformin dosage to 1000 mg twice daily. Start zoloft at 25 mg once daily. Continue albuterol two puffs every four to six hours as needed. Add symbicort two puffs twice a day during the summer. Schedule a follow-up in three months to review hemoglobin A1c and blood sugars.
